zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord William de Wilbraham於1246年出生在英國英格蘭爾拉德諾時，他的父親Richard De Wilburgham39歲，母親Letitia Venables16歲。他於1298年與Lady Christiana de Crosselegh在英國爾拉德諾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1339年在英國爾拉德諾享年93歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord William de Wilbraham于1246年出生在英国英格兰爾拉德諾时，他的父亲Richard De Wilburgham39岁，母亲Letitia Venables16岁。他于1298年与Lady Christiana de Crosselegh在英国爾拉德諾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1339年在英国爾拉德諾享年93岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wilbraham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Great and Little Wilbraham (Cambridgeshire). The placename derives from the Old English female personal name Wilburg + Old English hām ‘village, homestead’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord William de Wilbraham was born in 1246, in Radnor, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard De Wilburgham, was 39 and his mother, Letitia Venables, was 16. He married Lady Christiana de Crosselegh in 1298, in Radnor, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1339, in Radnor, Cheshire, England, at the age of 93.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord William de Wilbraham est né en 1246, à Radnor, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard De Wilburgham, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Letitia Venables, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Lady Christiana de Crosselegh en 1298, à Radnor, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1339, à Radnor, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 93 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord William de Wilbraham 1246 in Radnor, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard De Wilburgham 39 und seine Mutter Letitia Venables 16. Er hat 1298 in Radnor, Cheshire, England Lady Christiana de Crosselegh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1339 im Alter von 93 Jahren in Radnor, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord William de Wilbraham è nato il nel 1246 nel Radnor, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Richard De Wilburgham, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Letitia Venables, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Lady Christiana de Crosselegh nel 1298, nel Radnor, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1339, nel Radnor, Cheshire, England, all'età di 93 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord William de Wilbrahamが1246年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアラドノーで生まれた時，父Richard De Wilburghamは39歳，母Letitia Venablesは16歳であった。1298年にイギリスチェシュアイアラドノーでLady Christiana de Crosseleghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1339年にイギリスチェシュアイアラドノーで93歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord William de Wilbraham(이)가 1246년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 라드너에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard De Wilburgham(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Letitia Venables(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1298년 영국 체실르 라드너에서 Lady Christiana de Crosselegh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1339년 영국 체실르 라드너에서 93세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord William de Wilbraham nasceu em 1246, em Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard De Wilburgham, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Letitia Venables, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Christiana de Crosselegh em 1298, em Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1339, em Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 93 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord William de Wilbraham родился в 1246 г. в местности раднор, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard De Wilburgham было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Letitia Venables, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Christiana de Crosselegh в 1298 г. в местности раднор, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1339 г. в местности раднор, чешир, Англия в возрасте 93 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord William de Wilbraham nació en 1246, en Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard De Wilburgham, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Letitia Venables, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Lady Christiana de Crosselegh en 1298, en Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1339, en Radnor, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 93 años.
